known_gaps: The fixture generates the intended USB-C power input, protected VBUS path, 5 V to 3.3 V regulator rail, I2C sensor, pull-ups, decoupling, and header, but route-tree geometry still emits top-layer VCC_5v, VCC_3v3, and GND branches that cross or short local USB/regulator copper.

Required KiCad DRC currently reports clearance, shorting_items, solder_mask_bridge, and tracks_crossing violations, so the fixture must remain expected_fail until route-tree geometry or layer-access policy is repaired.
Internal connectivity validation also reports VCC_3v3 partial-route evidence from the same route-tree geometry blocker.
notes: Medium-complexity AI-generated KiCad-backed fixture combining USB-C power, protected 5 V input, 3.3 V regulator rail, I2C sensor, pull-ups, decoupling, and a header. This fixture is intentionally expected_fail until the remaining route-tree DRC/connectivity blockers are fixed and real KiCad ERC/DRC evidence is clean.
